description_long: This is a handheld two-way radio, commonly known as a walkie-talkie, with a robust black plastic casing and a prominent antenna extending from the top. The front features a small, illuminated LCD display showing channel information, flanked by several physical buttons for menu navigation and communication control. The lower section of the front panel has a textured speaker grille for audio output, and the brand name 'MIDLAND' is clearly printed below the display. The back side includes a battery compartment secured with screws and a belt clip for portability. The sides of the device have additional buttons and a textured grip for secure handling. The overall design is compact, rugged, and suitable for outdoor or urban use, often seen with security personnel, event staff, or in emergency scenarios.
description: A rugged black handheld walkie-talkie with an LCD display, physical buttons, and a prominent antenna. Features a speaker grille, belt clip, and textured grip for secure handling.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the battery compartment, belt clip, and screws securing the back panel.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the thickness, side buttons, and textured grip area.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the LCD screen, control buttons, speaker grille, and visible brand name.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile with additional grip texture and side features.
